description: Recto and verso: Last leaf of a letter from a woman to Sitt Khuṣrawān. In Judaeo-Arabic. Expressing deep regret that the addresse, whom she revered more than her own mother, was leaving the country. This regret was shared by Abū l-Fakhr b. al-Dimyāṭī. On the reverse side a male person addresses another male leaving the country. The same scribe wrote both sides; the hand is notable for large, beautiful letters and the substitution of shin for sin. The names Menashshe and Zayn are mentioned. (Information from Goitein's index card.)
description_he: עלה אחרון של מכתב מאשה לסת ח'וסראוואן. ביהודית-ערבית. מביעה צער עמוק על עזיבת הנמענת, שכיבדה אותה יותר מאמה שלה. בצד האחורי, פנייה של גבר לאחר העוזב את הארץ.
